title: hydrostachyales
intro: Hydrostachyales is an order of flowering plants, typically found in aquatic environments, characterized by their adaptation to wet conditions and often featuring long, slender stems and small, scale-like leaves. This order includes families such as the Hydrostachyaceae, which are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Other families within this order include Cyperaceae, known for their sedge-like plants, and Orchidaceae, one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Euphorbiaceae, a family known for its spurge plants, also belongs to the Hydrostachyales order.

families.family_desc: Vahliaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Malpighiales, commonly known as the vahlia family.
Hydatellaceae is a family of aquatic, parasitic flowering plants that are closely related to the water lilies.
